Title: Genome-Wide Identification and Analysis of the Ascorbate Peroxidase (APX) Gene Family of Winter Rapeseed (Brassica rapa L.) Under Abiotic Stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of five key BrAPX genes responsive to multiple abiotic stresses in Brassica rapa; selected from a genome-wide APX family (118 members) based on RNA-seq and qRT-PCR expression under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Bra035235 is significantly upregulated in growth cones of the cold-tolerant variety “Longyou-7” at 3 h of cold (4°C) stress and remains higher than in the weakly cold-resistant “Lenox”, indicating early and strong cold responsiveness.
2. Under cold stress, Bra035235 expression in “Longyou-7” growth cones is 4.1-, 6.6-, and 5.4-fold higher than in “Longyou-7” leaves, “Lenox” leaves, and roots, respectively, at 3 h, and 7.1-fold higher than in “Lenox” growth cones at 24 h.
3. Bra035235 is strongly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones under freezing (-4°C), drought (18% PEG6000), and heat (40°C) stresses, with transcript levels several- to >10-fold higher than in “Lenox”.
4. The multi-stress inducibility and higher expression in the cold-tolerant cultivar suggest Bra035235 is closely associated with abiotic stress responses and ROS-scavenging capacity in winter B. rapa.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (SRP179662, growth cones of B. rapa “Longyou-7” and “Lenox” under 4°C for 3 h and 24 h); RNA-seq (SRP211768, roots of B. rapa “Longyou-7” and “Tianyou-2” under -4°C for 6 h). Gene-specific expression validated by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-inducible APX gene whose transcript level is strongly elevated in the cold-tolerant cultivar under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses, indicating a close association with enhanced abiotic stress tolerance in winter rapeseed.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Functions as an ascorbate peroxidase family member involved in H2O2 detoxification within the ascorbate–glutathione antioxidant cycle, contributing to ROS scavenging under abiotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Antioxidant/ROS-scavenging pathway (ascorbate–glutathione cycle) and plant peroxidase-related stress-response pathway.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ascorbate–glutathione (Halliwell–Asada) cycle for hydrogen peroxide detoxification; associated with phenylpropanoid biosynthesis pathway based on STRING functional annotation.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ascorbate peroxidase (APX), plant peroxidase family (PF00141) containing conserved APX motifs identified by MEME.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern:
- Cold (4°C): Strongly upregulated in growth cones of “Longyou-7” at 3 h; expression much higher in “Longyou-7” than in “Lenox” and higher in growth cones than in leaves and roots; in leaves, continuously upregulated with higher levels at 24 h, especially in the cold-tolerant variety.
- Freezing (-4°C): Significantly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones; expression ~2-fold higher than in “Lenox” at 3 h.
- Drought (18% PEG6000): Strong induction in “Longyou-7” growth cones after 24 h, reaching ~13.4-fold higher expression than in “Lenox” growth cones.
- Heat (40°C): Markedly upregulated in growth cones and leaves of both cultivars, with higher expression in “Longyou-7” than in “Lenox”.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4°C (3 h and 24 h), freezing stress at -4°C, drought stress via 18% PEG6000 solution, and heat stress at 40°C applied to six-leaf-stage seedlings; tissues: growth cones and leaves of cultivars “Longyou-7” (ultra-cold-resistant) and “Lenox” (weakly cold-resistant).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and cold resistance mechanisms in winter Brassica rapa via APX gene family analysis.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, freezing, drought, and heat stress responses).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Brassica rapa winter-type varieties “Longyou-7” (ultra-cold-resistant) and “Lenox” (weakly cold-resistant), six-leaf-stage seedlings grown in pots; growth cones and leaves sampled under controlled abiotic stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide HMM search for peroxidase domain PF00141; motif and gene structure analysis (MEME, GSDS); phylogenetic analysis (ClustalW, MEGA); chromosomal mapping and duplication analysis (MCScanX, Circos); RNA-seq expression profiling under cold and freezing stress; qRT-PCR validation of gene expression under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses; STRING-based protein interaction and pathway prediction.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis from RNA-seq combined with qRT-PCR validation in contrasting cold-resistant cultivars under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Forward identification via genome-wide HMM domain (PF00141) search of the B. rapa genome followed by RNA-seq–based differential expression screening under cold and freezing stress, then qRT-PCR confirmation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Bra035235 is a strong multi-abiotic-stress-inducible APX gene with markedly higher expression in the ultra-cold-resistant cultivar “Longyou-7” than in the weakly cold-resistant “Lenox”, especially in growth cones under cold, freezing, drought, and heat. This makes Bra035235 a promising candidate marker or target gene for breeding winter B. rapa cultivars with enhanced cold and broad abiotic stress tolerance through marker-assisted selection or transgenic approaches.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as one of five key BrAPX genes closely related to abiotic stress responses in Brassica rapa; shows strong and sustained induction under cold and other stresses, particularly in the cold-tolerant cultivar.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Bra003918 is highly expressed in the cold-tolerant variety “Longyou-7” under cold (4°C) stress, with much higher transcript levels than in the weakly cold-resistant “Lenox”; it is one of the most strongly cold-induced BrAPX genes.
2. Under cold stress, Bra003918 expression in “Longyou-7” growth cones is significantly higher than in “Lenox” at both 3 h and 24 h, and is also induced in leaves, with expression at 24 h higher than at 3 h.
3. Bra003918 is significantly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones under freezing, drought, and heat stresses and is consistently more highly expressed than in “Lenox”, indicating broad abiotic stress responsiveness.
4. The combination of strong, sustained induction and cultivar-specific upregulation suggests Bra003918 plays an important role in the abiotic stress response and ROS homeostasis in winter B. rapa.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (SRP179662, growth cones of B. rapa “Longyou-7” and “Lenox” under 4°C for 3 h and 24 h) showing strong upregulation in the cold-tolerant cultivar; RNA-seq (SRP211768, roots under -4°C for 6 h) supporting expression under freezing stress; expression validated by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold- and multi-abiotic-stress-inducible APX gene with markedly higher expression in the cold-tolerant cultivar, closely associated with enhanced abiotic stress response and ROS-scavenging capacity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: As an APX family member, contributes to enzymatic scavenging of H2O2 in the ascorbate–glutathione antioxidant cycle during abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Antioxidant/ROS-scavenging (peroxisome-related) pathway and plant peroxidase-associated stress-response pathway.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ascorbate–glutathione (Halliwell–Asada) cycle for detoxification of reactive oxygen species.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ascorbate peroxidase (APX), member of the plant peroxidase (PF00141) gene family with conserved APX motifs.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern:
- Cold (4°C): Strongly upregulated in growth cones of “Longyou-7” at both 3 h and 24 h, with expression 2.9- and 3-fold higher than in “Lenox” growth cones, respectively; in leaves, continuously upregulated with higher expression in “Longyou-7”.
- Freezing (-4°C): Significantly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones, with expression ~4.3- and 2.2-fold higher than in “Lenox” growth cones at 3 h and 24 h, respectively; expression higher in growth cones than in leaves.
- Drought (18% PEG6000): Upregulated under drought stress, with higher expression in “Longyou-7” than in “Lenox”; in drought, Bra003918 shows relatively higher expression in leaves than in growth cones.
- Heat (40°C): Significantly upregulated in growth cones and leaves of both cultivars, with higher expression in “Longyou-7” than in “Lenox”.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4°C (3 h and 24 h), freezing at -4°C, drought via 18% PEG6000, and heat at 40°C applied to growth cones and leaves of cultivars “Longyou-7” and “Lenox”.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Molecular response of APX gene family members to abiotic stress and their role in cold resistance in winter Brassica rapa.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, freezing, drought, heat stress responses).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Brassica rapa winter-type cultivars “Longyou-7” (ultra-cold-resistant) and “Lenox” (weakly cold-resistant); six-leaf-stage seedlings; growth cones and leaves subjected to controlled abiotic stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide APX identification, RNA-seq expression profiling in growth cones and roots under cold and freezing stress, and qRT-PCR validation under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses; bioinformatic pathway and interaction prediction via STRING.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression validated by RNA-seq and qRT-PCR in contrasting cold-resistant cultivars under multiple abiotic stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from a genome-wide APX gene family scan using HMM of the peroxidase domain, then prioritized as a core gene based on RNA-seq differential expression under cold/freezing and qRT-PCR confirmation under multiple stresses.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because Bra003918 is strongly and consistently induced by cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses and is more highly expressed in the ultra-cold-resistant cultivar, it represents a promising candidate for improving multi-abiotic stress tolerance in winter B. rapa through marker-assisted selection or targeted genetic modification.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of five key BrAPX genes responsive to abiotic stress; shows robust induction particularly in growth cones of the cold-tolerant cultivar under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stress.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Bra033040 is significantly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones under cold (4°C) stress and exhibits much higher expression than in “Lenox”, especially at 24 h.
2. Under cold stress, Bra033040 expression in “Longyou-7” growth cones is 10.8- and 34.5-fold higher than in “Lenox” growth cones at 3 h and 24 h, respectively; in leaves, it is continuously upregulated with higher levels in “Longyou-7”.
3. Bra033040 is strongly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones under freezing (-4°C), drought, and heat stresses, with expression exceeding that in “Lenox” by several- to many-fold, indicating broad abiotic stress responsiveness.
4. Its strong growth-cone-biased induction and cultivar-specific upregulation indicate Bra033040 is an important APX gene associated with abiotic stress responses in winter B. rapa.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (SRP179662) showing differential expression in growth cones under cold stress; RNA-seq (SRP211768) for roots under freezing stress; gene-specific patterns confirmed by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly stress-inducible APX gene with large-fold induction in the cold-tolerant cultivar, particularly in growth cones, closely associated with cold, freezing, drought, and heat stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as an APX family peroxidase involved in scavenging H2O2 via the ascorbate–glutathione antioxidant system during abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Antioxidant/ROS-scavenging pathway linked to peroxisome-related stress responses; categorized within plant peroxidase-related pathways.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Participates in the ascorbate–glutathione (Halliwell–Asada) cycle for ROS detoxification.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ascorbate peroxidase (APX) in the plant peroxidase (PF00141) family, containing conserved APX motifs identified by MEME.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern:
- Cold (4°C): In growth cones of “Longyou-7”, expression is 10.8-fold higher than in “Lenox” at 3 h and 34.5-fold higher at 24 h; in leaves, continuously upregulated during cold stress with higher levels in “Longyou-7”.
- Freezing (-4°C): In “Longyou-7” growth cones, expression is about 3.4-fold higher than in “Lenox” at 3 h and 6-fold higher at 24 h; expression higher in growth cones than in leaves.
- Drought (18% PEG6000): After 24 h, significantly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones, ~3.4-fold higher than in “Lenox”.
- Heat (40°C): Strong induction in growth cones and leaves of both cultivars, with consistently higher levels in “Longyou-7”.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold (4°C), freezing (-4°C), drought (18% PEG6000), and heat (40°C) stress applied at the six-leaf stage to growth cones and leaves of “Longyou-7” and “Lenox”.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of APX gene family members, including Bra033040, in abiotic stress and cold resistance in winter Brassica rapa.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, freezing, drought, heat).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Winter-type B. rapa cultivars “Longyou-7” and “Lenox”; six-leaf-stage seedlings grown in pots; growth cones and leaves harvested after abiotic stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide APX identification, RNA-seq profiling under cold and freezing, and qRT-PCR expression analysis under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses; interaction and pathway prediction with STRING.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression changes validated by RNA-seq and qRT-PCR in contrasting cold-resistance cultivars under multiple abiotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified via genome-wide HMM scan for APX genes and selected as a key gene based on RNA-seq differential expression and qRT-PCR validation under abiotic stress conditions.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: The very strong induction of Bra033040 in growth cones and leaves of the ultra-cold-resistant cultivar under cold, freezing, drought, and heat makes it an attractive candidate gene for breeding winter B. rapa varieties with improved multi-stress tolerance using marker-assisted selection or functional engineering.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Selected as one of five key BrAPX genes; exhibits sustained and strong induction in the cold-tolerant cultivar under multiple abiotic stresses, indicating involvement in generalized stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Bra017120 is significantly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones under cold (4°C) stress, with higher expression than in “Lenox” at both 3 h and 24 h.
2. Under cold stress, Bra017120 expression in “Longyou-7” growth cones is 2.4- and 4.4-fold higher than in “Lenox” at 3 h and 24 h, respectively; its expression is also induced in leaves and is higher in “Longyou-7”.
3. Bra017120 is markedly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones under freezing (-4°C), drought, and heat stresses; under freezing at 3 h, expression is ~2.9-fold higher than in “Lenox”, and under drought at 24 h, ~32.9-fold higher; under heat, expression in “Longyou-7” growth cones is 12.2- and 2.7-fold higher than in “Lenox” at 3 h and 24 h, respectively.
4. These patterns indicate Bra017120 is a highly responsive APX gene associated with cold and broad abiotic stress responses in winter B. rapa.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets (SRP179662 and SRP211768) show induction of Bra017120 under cold and freezing stress; qRT-PCR confirms strong upregulation under cold, freezing, drought, and heat in contrasting cultivars.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong and sustained stress-inducible APX gene whose expression is consistently higher in the cold-tolerant cultivar across cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses, implicating it in enhanced abiotic stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as part of the APX-mediated ROS-scavenging system, reducing H2O2 via the ascorbate–glutathione cycle during abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Antioxidant/ROS-scavenging pathway associated with peroxidase activity under abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ascorbate peroxidase (APX), member of the plant peroxidase (PF00141) family with conserved APX motifs.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern:
- Cold (4°C): In “Longyou-7” growth cones, expression is 2.4- and 4.4-fold higher than in “Lenox” at 3 h and 24 h, respectively; in leaves, continuously upregulated with higher levels in “Longyou-7”.
- Freezing (-4°C): Significantly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones, with ~2.9-fold higher expression than in “Lenox” at 3 h.
- Drought (18% PEG6000): Strongly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones after 24 h, ~32.9-fold higher than in “Lenox”.
- Heat (40°C): Markedly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones, with 12.2- and 2.7-fold higher expression than in “Lenox” at 3 h and 24 h, respectively; also upregulated in leaves with higher levels in “Longyou-7”.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold (4°C), freezing (-4°C), drought (18% PEG6000), and heat (40°C) stresses applied to growth cones and leaves of “Longyou-7” and “Lenox”.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Molecular characterization of APX genes, including Bra017120, in the abiotic stress and cold resistance responses of winter Brassica rapa.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, freezing, drought, heat).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Winter-type B. rapa cultivars “Longyou-7” and “Lenox”; six-leaf-stage seedlings in pots; growth cones and leaves collected after abiotic stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide APX identification, RNA-seq profiling under cold and freezing, and qRT-PCR analysis under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses; interaction network and pathway prediction using STRING.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq–based differential expression coupled with qRT-PCR validation in contrasting cold-resistant cultivars under multiple abiotic stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the genome-wide APX family (PF00141 domain) and selected as a key gene based on strong RNA-seq and qRT-PCR responses to abiotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Bra017120’s strong induction across cold, freezing, drought, and heat in the ultra-cold-resistant cultivar suggests it is a valuable candidate gene for breeding winter B. rapa with improved multi-abiotic stress tolerance, potentially useful as a marker or engineering target.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Recognized as one of five key BrAPX genes closely related to abiotic stress responses; shows prominent expression in leaves of the cold-tolerant cultivar, especially under cold and drought.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Bra031934 is significantly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones at 3 h of cold (4°C) stress and remains induced in leaves, with higher expression in “Longyou-7” than in “Lenox”.
2. In leaves under cold stress, Bra031934 is among the most highly expressed BrAPX genes in “Longyou-7”, with 2.3-fold higher expression than in “Lenox” leaves at 24 h.
3. Bra031934 is strongly induced in “Longyou-7” growth cones under freezing and drought stresses, with expression ~10-fold higher than in “Lenox” growth cones after 24 h of drought; it is also induced by heat stress, with higher expression in “Longyou-7”.
4. These patterns indicate Bra031934 contributes to the abiotic stress-responsive APX network, particularly in leaves, and is associated with stress tolerance in winter B. rapa.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (SRP179662) expression profiles under cold stress in growth cones; qRT-PCR validation under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses; additional RNA-seq (SRP211768) for roots under freezing.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: APX gene strongly induced by cold, freezing, drought, and heat, particularly in leaves of the cold-tolerant cultivar, supporting a role in abiotic stress response and ROS management.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Functions as part of the APX-mediated H2O2 scavenging system (ascorbate–glutathione cycle) under abiotic stress conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Antioxidant/ROS-scavenging pathway related to peroxidase activity in response to abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ascorbate peroxidase (APX), plant peroxidase (PF00141) family member with conserved APX motifs.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern:
- Cold (4°C): Significantly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones at 3 h; in leaves, Bra031934 shows strong induction and is 2.3-fold more highly expressed in “Longyou-7” than in “Lenox” at 24 h.
- Freezing (-4°C): One of five APX genes significantly expressed in “Longyou-7” under freezing, with higher transcript levels than in “Lenox”.
- Drought (18% PEG6000): After 24 h, markedly upregulated in “Longyou-7” growth cones, ~10-fold higher than in “Lenox” growth cones.
- Heat (40°C): Significantly upregulated in growth cones and leaves of both cultivars, with higher expression in “Longyou-7” than in “Lenox”.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold (4°C), freezing (-4°C), drought (18% PEG6000), and heat (40°C) stress at the six-leaf stage applied to growth cones and leaves of “Longyou-7” and “Lenox”.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of APX gene family members, including Bra031934, in leaf and growth-cone responses to abiotic stress in winter Brassica rapa.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, freezing, drought, heat).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Winter B. rapa cultivars “Longyou-7” (ultra-cold-resistant) and “Lenox” (weakly cold-resistant); six-leaf-stage seedlings; growth cones and leaves used for expression assays under abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide APX family identification, RNA-seq profiling under cold and freezing, and qRT-PCR expression analysis of selected APX genes under cold, freezing, drought, and heat stresses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression validation by RNA-seq and qRT-PCR in contrasting cold-resistance cultivars under multiple abiotic stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the genome-wide APX gene family and selected as a key abiotic stress-responsive gene based on RNA-seq and qRT-PCR data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Given its strong induction in leaves and growth cones of the ultra-cold-resistant cultivar under cold, freezing, drought, and heat, Bra031934 is a promising candidate gene for developing winter B. rapa lines with improved abiotic stress tolerance via marker-assisted breeding or functional manipulation.
